title: parathelypteris glanduligera
intro: Parathelypteris glanduligera, also known as the glandular chain fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. It is native to Asia, particularly in China, Japan, and the Philippines, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and it is also believed to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties that can help to boost the immune system. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
